steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage lists neighborhood type and individual fitness as factors studies emphasize, then signals with "multiple conditions and needs inform individuals' choices" that these are only some of many factors. The right answer must capture both what the examples represent and the passage's broader complexity point.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — They are examples of factors that studies suggest are important in people's decision-making about walking but that the text claims most people rarely consider when making walking decisions.: Contradicts the passage, which treats these as genuinely important influences. ✗
  • Choice B — They represent factors that have been identified as important influences on walking decisions but that the text suggests are merely some of the many factors that may contribute to people's decision-making about walking.: Captures both aspects — what studies identify as influential and the text's emphasis that more factors exist. ✓
  • Choice C — They illustrate factors that researchers believe people consider when making walking decisions in most contexts but that the text argues are unique to walking decisions made by people in Lisbon.: Narrows the claim to one location when the passage makes a general point. ✗
  • Choice D — They identify factors that Mendes argues have been overemphasized in studies of decision-making about walking but that the text asserts are relevant to most people's walking decisions.: Misrepresents the passage's argument about walking decisions. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
